title: B.B.A. (Hons. with Research) Financial Investment Analysis
content: BBA (FIA) is a specialized program that has been designed to provide students with a strong foundation in business and management principles along with conceptual, practical, and analytical skills for financial and investment decision-making. Students will gain specialized expertise in various facets of Financial and Investment Analysis, including Financial Analytics, Financial Management, Portfolio and Investment Analysis, Investment Management, Financial Derivatives, International Finance, and Accounting. This comprehensive training equips them with the ability to make informed financial decisions in diverse and dynamic environments, both locally and globally. Upon completion, graduates will be well-prepared to excel as finance professionals and entrepreneurs, navigating complex financial landscapes with confidence and strategic acumen. Main specialized courses:- Skill based courses:- Value added courses:- XII with minimum 50% marks in Aggregate. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list For Migration from Other Universities: 1) BBA (FIA) Ist yr. minimum 50% marks or 5.0 CGPA on a 10 point scale 2) 50% or above in best four relevant subjects at XII level 01 Apply the knowledge of finance and management theories to solve real business issues. 02 Apply knowledge related to investment analysis in various projects as an entrepreneur/a corporate executive to manage projects in multidisciplinary environments. 03 Analyze and explore alternate solutions for financial and economical issues at national and international level for reaching validated conclusions. 04 Apply appropriate modern IT tools, techniques and resources including prediction & financial modelling for business analysis. 05 Apply the contextual knowledge to assess the issues related to financial inclusion and investment alternatives pertaining to business management practices. 06 07 Develop the students with requisite knowledge, skills & right attitude necessary to create effective leadership that enables students to behave ethically and responsibly. 08 Communicate effectively on complex business management activities with the industry and society at large. 01 Equip students to make a positive contribution to business, trade and industry at both national and global levels. 02 Students should possess the ability to analyze complex business problems, think critically, and apply analytical skills to make informed decisions in diverse organizational contexts of financial management and other related areas. 03 Graduates shall drive entrepreneurship initiatives fostering self-employment and have the aptitude and ability to engage in independent and lifelong learning of socio-economic, technological and global change. 04 Students should recognize their social responsibility and contribute positively to their communities. This involves an understanding of the impact of business activities on society and the environment. 01 Understand the holistic perspective of financial environment to identify, formulate, and analyze financial issues in the national and international context. 02 Perform an In-depth Financial analysis by using appropriate technical knowledge, theoretical knowledge and customized financial software. XII with minimum 50% marks in Aggregate. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list For Migration from Other Universities: 1) BBA (FIA) Ist yr. minimum 50% marks or 5.0 CGPA on a 10 point scale 2) 50% or above in best four relevant subjects at XII level
